articles.title: Uncle Sam is wrong, India and China are doing their bit to fight climate change
articles.summary: It is true that there is an increase in carbon emission by India and China due to homegrown consumption and lack of infrastructure. But let s keep in mind that India s carbon emissions per capita are still 10 times lower and China s is half of that compared to the US.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Nifty IT jumps over 2.5% as strong US software earnings revive sector optimism
articles.summary: Domestic technology stocks saw significant gains on June 1, with all Nifty IT index constituents rising. Strong US software earnings and improved sentiment boosted confidence, reversing previous losses. Persistent Systems led with a 4% increase, while the Nifty IT index closed up 2.66%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger small-cap stock surges 19% to 52-week high despite stock market crash; here's why

articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 1: PTC Industries, NMDC Steel, Saregama India, Welspun Living, among top gainers
articles.summary: The Indian stock market closed sharply lower on June 1, with the Nifty 50 and Sensex both down 0.74%. Selling pressure from foreign investors and weak global cues impacted FMCG, financial, and auto stocks, while Nifty IT gained 2.62% amid broader market declines.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Sensex crashes 500 points, extends losing run to 4th day; what drove the Indian stock market lower?

articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: NMDC Steel shares jump 18% after Q4 profit turnaround; hits 20-month high
articles.summary: NMDC Steel shares surged 18% to ₹52.60, hitting a 24-month high after reporting a strong financial turnaround for Q4FY26, with a net profit of ₹391.9 crore and revenue growth of 36.7%. For FY26, net profit was ₹58.7 crore, reversing previous losses.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Do you know what ADRs are? A complete guide for investors
articles.summary: American Depositary Receipts enable US investors to easily buy shares of foreign companies. While once popular among Indian investors, newer investment avenues have reduced ADRs' significance, which now cater to specialised needs such as tracking price differences and specific asset allocations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.summary: Zee Entertainment's stock surged after announcing a partnership with FIFA for broadcasting the 2026 and 2030 World Cups in India. The deal, reportedly between $30-35 million, comes 10 days ahead of the tournament.
articles.label: BULLISH
